description: Controls warmup/active/frozen phase behavior for adaptive routing training
help_md:
### Technical
Gates gradient flow based on training phase for adaptive routing components.

Phases:
- Warmup (step < warmup_steps): passthrough, routing params frozen
- Active (step >= warmup_steps): normal gradient flow
- Frozen: x.detach() to stop gradients

Inputs: x:tensor[B, S, D].
Outputs: y:tensor[B, S, D].
Params: warmup_steps (integer).

### Plain Language
Manages the training schedule for adaptive routing, ensuring the model learns
basic representations before the routing mechanism kicks in.
name: Training Phase Gate

warmup_steps.description: Number of warmup steps before activating routing

description: Joins two row-like tensors with inner/left/outer/concat-key semantics using configurable key columns.
help_md:
### Technical
Performs relational-style joins on row-like tensors.

Inputs:
- `left:tensor[*]`
- `right:tensor[*]`

Output:
- `joined:tensor[*]`

Supported modes:
- `inner`: matched key rows only
- `left`: all left rows + matched right rows (fill for missing)
- `outer`: union of both keys with fill for unmatched side
- `concat_key`: key-group concatenation with fill for missing side

Notes:
- Inputs are normalized to row format `(N, F)` for joining.
- `key_tolerance` allows approximate numeric key matching.
- `max_matches_per_key` caps duplicate-key cross-products.

### Plain Language
Use this to combine two datasets/tensors by ID (or another key column), similar to SQL joins.
Pick `inner` when you only want shared keys, `left` when left is the source of truth, and `outer`
when you want to keep everything from both sides.
name: Join

expected_left_dim.description: Expected left feature dimension in strict schema mode (-1 disables check).

expected_right_dim.description: Expected right feature dimension in strict schema mode (-1 disables check).

include_key_columns.description: Keep key columns from both sides in the joined output.

join_mode.description: Join behavior. `concat_key` concatenates rows grouped by key with zero-fill for missing side.

key_tolerance.description: Numeric key tolerance. 0 means exact match.

left_key_index.description: Key column index for left input.

max_matches_per_key.description: Safety cap to prevent combinatorial explosion for duplicate keys.

missing_fill_value.description: Fill value for unmatched side in left/outer/concat_key modes.

right_key_index.description: Key column index for right input.

schema_validation.description: Strict mode enforces expected feature dims for left/right inputs.

Projects row-like tensor data onto selected feature columns.

Provide schema column names once, then choose columns by name in the Inspector.

drop_invalid.description: Drop unknown names/out-of-range indices instead of failing.

keep_order.description: Preserve user-specified column ordering; otherwise use sorted unique order.

schema_columns.description: Comma-separated column names that define the available schema (for name-based selection).

schema_validation.description: Strict mode fails when requested columns are not present in schema/data.

selected_columns.description: Comma-separated column names to keep. In Inspector this is shown as a schema-aware multi-select list.

selection_mode.description: Choose whether to select columns by schema names or numeric indices.

description: Deterministically splits tabular/tensor rows into train, validation, and test subsets with optional stratification.
help_md:
### Technical
Deterministically splits row-like data into train/val/test tensors.

Inputs: `data:tensor[*]`.
Outputs: `train:tensor[*]`, `val:tensor[*]`, `test:tensor[*]`.

Features:
- deterministic splits via `seed`
- optional stratification via `stratify=true` and `stratify_col`
- configurable ratio triplet (`train_ratio`, `val_ratio`, `test_ratio`)

### Plain Language
Use this before model training to create reproducible train/validation/test subsets.
Turn on stratification when you want each split to keep similar class balance.
name: Split Train/Val/Test

expected_feature_dim.description: Expected feature dimension for schema validation (-1 disables check).

schema_validation.description: Strict mode enforces expected_feature_dim when set.

seed.description: Deterministic random seed used by shuffling/stratification.

shuffle.description: Shuffle rows before splitting (deterministic with seed).

stratify.description: Preserve approximate class distribution using values from stratify_col.

stratify_bins.description: Number of bins for continuous stratification labels.

stratify_col.description: Feature column index used to derive stratification labels.

test_ratio.description: Fraction of rows assigned to the test split.

train_ratio.description: Fraction of rows assigned to the training split.

val_ratio.description: Fraction of rows assigned to the validation split.

description: Auxiliary loss enforcing balanced lane utilization via L2(actual_fracs - target_fracs)
help_md:
### Technical
Computes auxiliary loss to encourage balanced utilization across lanes.

Loss = loss_weight * L2(actual_lane_fractions - target_fractions).
Target fractions default to uniform (1/num_lanes each).
Loss is stashed as _aux_loss on the module for the training loop to pick up.

Inputs: x:tensor[B, S, D].
Outputs: y:tensor[B, S, D] (passthrough).
Params: num_lanes (integer), loss_weight (float).

### Plain Language
Encourages the router to send roughly equal numbers of tokens to each lane,
preventing all tokens from being routed to a single easy lane.
name: Load Balance Loss

loss_weight.description: Weight for the auxiliary balance loss

description: 2-layer MLP scoring per-token difficulty (D→D//8→1, sigmoid output)
help_md:
### Technical
2-layer MLP that scores per-token difficulty for adaptive routing.

Architecture: Linear(D→D//8) → ReLU → Linear(D//8→1) → Sigmoid.
Returns input tensor unchanged (passthrough); scores are stashed for downstream routing ops.

Inputs: x:tensor[B, S, D].
Outputs: y:tensor[B, S, D].

### Plain Language
Assigns each token a difficulty score between 0 and 1, used by downstream
routing components to decide which processing lane handles each token.
name: Difficulty Scorer

description: Produces routing signal based on entropy of input scores.
help_md:
### Technical Computes per-token Shannon entropy over a set of categorical scores (e.g. from an MoE router or classifier). High entropy indicates uncertainty.
Inputs: scores:tensor[B, S, K]. Outputs: routing_signal:tensor[B, S, 1]. ### Plain Language Measures how "uncertain" the model is about a token. Can be used to route uncertain tokens to more powerful compute paths.
name: Entropy Router

description: Threshold/learned routing — maps difficulty scores to lane assignments and weights
help_md:
### Technical
Routes tokens to processing lanes based on upstream difficulty scores.

Threshold mode: normalizes scores to [0,1] and uses torch.bucketize to assign
tokens to N equally-spaced lanes. Creates one-hot weight tensor [B,S,N].

Inputs: x:tensor[B, S, D].
Outputs: y:tensor[B, S, D].
Params: num_lanes (integer), routing_mode (string).

### Plain Language
Decides which processing lane each token should go to based on its difficulty score.
name: Lane Router

description: Splits batch by lane assignment. Phase A uses dense passthrough (all lanes get full tensor)
help_md:
### Technical
Dispatches tokens to a specific processing lane based on routing assignments.

Phase A (current): dense passthrough — returns input unchanged.
Phase B (future): sparse dispatch via C kernels with variable-size lane buffers.

Each lane gets a separate conditional_dispatch node with lane=0/1/2 config.

Inputs: x:tensor[B, S, D].
Outputs: y:tensor[B, S, D].
Params: num_lanes (integer), lane (integer).

### Plain Language
Sends tokens to a specific processing lane. Currently passes everything through;
future versions will do actual sparse routing.
name: Conditional Dispatch

lane.description: Which lane this dispatch node feeds

description: Reassembles lane outputs with routing weights. Phase A uses weighted average of 2 inputs
help_md:
### Technical
Gathers and combines outputs from multiple processing lanes.

Phase A (current): equal-weight average of 2 inputs.
Phase B (future): weighted combination using routing weights from lane_router.

For 3 lanes, use 2 chained gather nodes: gather(lane0, lane1) → gather(result, lane2).

Inputs: a:tensor[B, S, D], b:tensor[B, S, D].
Outputs: y:tensor[B, S, D].
Params: num_lanes (integer).

### Plain Language
Combines the outputs from different processing lanes back into a single tensor.
name: Conditional Gather
